rules.message: AES ECB mode leaks plaintext patterns and should not be used for sensitive data.
ECB mode object detected; ECB should not be used for sensitive data.
Legacy block cipher detected; migrate to AES or ChaCha20.
Deprecated hash algorithm detected; use SHA-256, SHA-512, or SHA3-256.
RSA key size below 2048 bits detected; use at least 2048 bits, prefer 4096.
RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 padding is vulnerable to padding oracle attacks; use OAEP instead.
PBKDF2 iteration count appears low for modern password-derived key material; use ≥100000.
Non-cryptographic random source detected; use os.urandom() or secrets module for crypto.
String literal used near a cryptographic call; review for hardcoded secrets or keys.
CBC mode requires proper authentication, random IV handling, and padding; review surrounding code.
ChaCha20 detected; critical that nonce/IV is never reused with the same key.

rules.note: AEAD mode detected; verify proper nonce handling and tag verification.
CTR mode detected; ensure IV is unique per key and never reused.
Argon2 detected (recommended password hashing algorithm); verify appropriate memory/time parameters.

rules.message: Scrypt detected; ensure cost parameters (N, r, p) are appropriate for the use case.

rules.note: bcrypt detected; verify cost factor is at least 12 for password hashing.
Fernet (high-level symmetric encryption) detected; good for most use cases.

rules.message: HMAC detected; ensure hash algorithm is SHA-256 or stronger, not MD5 or SHA-1.
JWT detected; verify signature validation is enabled and algorithm is not 'none'.
ECC detected; verify strong curves are used (SECP256R1 or stronger).
DSA detected; prefer ECDSA with strong curves (SECP256R1 or SECP384R1).
X.509 certificate creation detected; ensure proper validation chain and expiration.
Key material may be logged; ensure sensitive keys are not printed or logged.

rules.note: HKDF detected; verify salt and info parameters are appropriate for the context.

rules.message: Pickle detected with cryptographic objects; pickle is insecure; use JSON with PEM encoding.
Hardcoded key/secret detected; use environment variables or secure key management.
IV/nonce generated insecurely; must use cryptographically secure RNG (os.urandom, secrets).
Weak random source used for IV; use os.urandom() or secrets module.
